evals.prompt: I want to use open-source AI models but I don't have a Chutes account yet. Can you set me up from scratch and give me an API key I can start using right away?
evals.expected_output: Should walk through account registration via API or web, warn about fingerprint sensitivity, offer to create a credential backup file, then create an API key and present it clearly. Should mention recovery options.

evals.prompt: What DeepSeek models are available on Chutes and how much do they cost? I need something that supports tool calling for my agent framework.
evals.expected_output: Should query or reference the live models list, filter for DeepSeek models, show pricing, and specifically check the supported_features field for 'tools' support. Should present a clear recommendation and prefer https://llm.chutes.ai/v1/models as source of truth for current metadata.

evals.prompt: I have a Chutes API key already (cpk_abc123). Show me how to call a model from Python and also check how much balance I have left.
evals.expected_output: Should show Python code using OpenAI SDK pointed at llm.chutes.ai/v1, and show how to check balance via GET /users/me. Should use the provided key in examples.

evals.prompt: I'm building a chatbot and need it to be fast and reliable. Can you set up model routing on Chutes so if one model is slow or down, it automatically fails over? I want the lowest latency possible and I care about privacy so TEE models only.
evals.expected_output: Should explain model routing strategies (default, default:latency, default:throughput), recommend using default:latency for a chatbot, note that as of 2026-06-11 every hosted model is TEE-backed (confidential_compute=true) so the TEE-only constraint is satisfied by the whole catalog, build a routing pool of 3-4 fast models, and provide working code examples using either inline routing strings or the default alias.

evals.prompt: I need hardware-isolated inference for sensitive prompts. How do I identify which Chutes models are actually TEE-backed, and how should I verify that?
evals.expected_output: Should state that confidential_compute=true from https://llm.chutes.ai/v1/models is the canonical indicator, not the -TEE suffix alone. Should mention attestation evidence endpoints and explain how to use live model metadata as the source of truth.

evals.prompt: What are my options for topping up Chutes, and how do I check whether the Harvard research discount is active on my account?
evals.expected_output: Should explain crypto top-ups via payment_address, mention Stripe top-up path, and show how to call GET /users/me/discounts or equivalent account endpoints to verify active discounts.

evals.prompt: I've just created a Chutes account and API key. Help me store them securely so my agent can reuse them later without dumping secrets into files or chat.
evals.expected_output: Should direct the user toward the secure credential manager, explain keychain/secret-service/fallback encrypted storage, and avoid recommending plaintext backups as the primary storage path.

evals.prompt: I use Hermes and want Chutes available there right now, even before first-class provider support lands. How should I configure it?
evals.expected_output: Should explain Hermes named custom-provider setup, mention CHUTES_API_KEY, provide a config.yaml example, and distinguish the normal Chutes endpoint from the research endpoint. Should not pretend Chutes is already a built-in Hermes provider unless that is actually true in the runtime being discussed.

evals.prompt: I want to use GPT as my main Hermes model but send cheaper delegated/background work to Chutes. What would that config look like?
evals.expected_output: Should explain a split-brain Hermes setup using Chutes as delegation and/or cheap model routing, include example config snippets, and mention that exact provider keys may depend on Hermes custom-provider naming in the installed version.

evals.prompt: When should I use the Chutes Harvard research endpoint instead of the normal endpoint?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the discount tradeoff clearly: same auth and API shape, but prompts/responses may be recorded for research. Should advise against using it for sensitive/private workloads without explicit consent.

evals.prompt: Add Sign in with Chutes to my Next.js App Router app at ~/projects/my-app. I want the user to sign in with their Chutes account and have my server make inference calls on their behalf.
evals.expected_output: Should route to the chutes-sign-in skill (BETA). Should detect Next.js App Router, register an OAuth app via POST /idp/apps with scopes openid profile chutes:invoke, store client_id and client_secret in the OS keychain under a named profile via manage_credentials.py, vendor the upstream chutesai/Sign-in-with-Chutes Next.js package (lib/, hooks/, app/api/auth/chutes/, components/SignInButton.tsx) into the target, append CHUTES_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID / CHUTES_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET / NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL to .env.local, print the layout diff for the SignInButton WITHOUT auto-editing layout files, and verify via POST /idp/token/introspect once a session exists. Must never echo csc_... into the conversation. Must acknowledge BETA status.

evals.prompt: Deploy Qwen/Qwen3-8B as a vLLM chute on an H100 and give it a stable alias called 'interactive-fast' so my team can point at the alias instead of hardcoding the model id.
evals.expected_output: Should route to the chutes-deploy skill (permanent BETA). Should explicitly acknowledge that deploy consumes real paid compute, confirm before POSTing, POST /chutes/vllm with a templated body (model Qwen/Qwen3-8B, gpu_type h100, gpu_count 1, revision pinned to a full 40-hex HF commit SHA — the API rejects branch names and short SHAs), stream build logs from GET /images/{image_id}/logs, poll GET /chutes/warmup/{chute_id} until ready, then POST /model_aliases/ with {alias: interactive-fast, chute_ids: [<chute_uuid>]} — NOT {alias, model}. Should return the base_url + model_id for inference. Must warn about BETA status and that the easy-deploy lane may be server-side gated (403) on some account classes.

evals.prompt: Make Chutes available in Cursor via MCP. I use the repo-level .cursor/mcp.json. Also run a quick health check when you're done.
evals.expected_output: Should route to the chutes-mcp-portability skill (BETA). Should install the chutes-mcp-server via uv tool install (from plugins/chutes-ai/skills/chutes-mcp-portability/mcp-server), run generate_agent_config.py --target cursor to write .cursor/mcp.json, ensure CHUTES_API_KEY is exported from the keychain via manage_credentials.py, and run chutes-mcp-server --self-check (which calls chutes_list_models with limit=1). Must never embed a raw cpk_ value into the config file; must use ${env:CHUTES_API_KEY}. Must acknowledge that read-only MCP tools graduate out of BETA after a verified call but write/deploy MCP tools stay BETA permanently.

evals.prompt: Rotate our Chutes OAuth client secret for the 'oauth.my-app' profile without breaking production.
evals.expected_output: Should route to chutes-sign-in:rotate_secret.py. Should read the app_id UUID (NOT the client_id) from the keychain profile, POST /idp/apps/{app_id}/regenerate-secret, write the new client_secret back to the same keychain profile, and print a prominent redeploy reminder listing Vercel / Docker / bare-metal update steps. Must never auto-redeploy. Must never print the old or new secret value (only a redacted preview). If the keychain write fails after the secret rotated, must print a CRITICAL warning and exit non-zero. If the profile is legacy-shaped and has no app_id, must print a clear migration hint.

evals.prompt: Build me a routing pool for interactive-fast and pin it as an alias. Use the smallest reasonable pool.
evals.expected_output: Should route to chutes-routing:build_pool.py. Should query live /v1/models, apply the interactive-fast filter (text I/O, cheapest-first), take top N (3-4), print the models with prompt/completion prices, emit an inline routing string with the :latency suffix, and (if --alias is requested) POST /model_aliases/ with {alias, chute_ids:[...]} - NOT {alias, model}. Should not pretend /v1/models has average_ttft/average_tps; client-side ranks by cost, server-side :latency ranks by TTFT.

evals.prompt: What's my current Chutes spend and am I anywhere near a cap?
evals.expected_output: Should route to chutes-usage-and-billing:spend_summary.py + quota_guard.py. Must pull balance from /users/me, subscription caps (four_hour + monthly) from /users/me/subscription_usage, discounts from /users/me/discounts, price overrides from /users/me/price_overrides, and TAO rate from /fmv. Must clearly label these as PERSONAL data and NOT confuse them with /payments/summary/tao (which is platform-wide TAO deposits, not the user's bill). Must show ASCII progress bars against caps and warn at 70/85/95 percent.

evals.prompt: Audit my OAuth apps - I want to see which ones I can clean up.
evals.expected_output: Should route to chutes-platform-ops:list_apps.py + audit_stale_apps.py. Must paginate /idp/apps (2026-06-11 findings: ?mine=true is still IGNORED by the server, but ?include_public=false now scopes server-side to your own apps and ?search= filters by name; ?user_id=<other-uuid> returns 403; client-side filtering on user_id remains the safe fallback), join with /idp/authorizations to compute per-app auth counts, and print a report flagging zero-authorization apps and apps aged >= 90 days. Must NOT delete anything - report only. Must exit with code 3 when stale apps are found.

evals.prompt: I'm building autonomous agent infrastructure and each agent should register its own Chutes account. Show me how to do a dry run of agent registration first.
evals.expected_output: Should route to chutes-agent-registration:register_agent.py with --dry-run (BETA). Must explain the agent-vs-human on-ramp tradeoff before proceeding, print the POST /users/agent_registration body shape {hotkey, coldkey, signature} with the signature REDACTED, and stop. Must NOT attempt a live POST. Must explicitly mention that both --yes AND --i-know-bittensor flags are required for a real registration and that real registration has on-chain implications. Must never auto-generate a signature.

evals.prompt: I'm using a confidential_compute=true chute for sensitive prompts. Verify that the attestation evidence actually looks right.
evals.expected_output: Should route to chutes-tee:attest_chute.py. Must GET /instances/nonce or generate a 64-hex nonce via secrets.token_hex(32), then GET /chutes/{id}/evidence?nonce=<nonce> (wave-2 finding: nonce query param is REQUIRED and must be exactly 64 hex chars), parse the TDX v4 quote header (version=4, tee_type=0x81, att_key_type=2), extract mrtd + rtmr[0..3] + report_data, parse one NVIDIA GPU certificate (arch is fleet-dependent - Blackwell observed live 2026-06-11, Hopper previously; do not hardcode), optionally cross-check mrtd/rtmr0 against the public GET /servers/tee/measurements golden sets, and return a verdict of 'shape-valid' if crypto validation isn't available (explicitly labeled 'not run - install Intel DCAP for verified mode'). Must NOT claim 'verified' unless DCAP tooling actually ran. Must explain honest limits of TEE (see references/what-tee-does-not-protect.md).

evals.prompt: I want to run a Codex-style coding agent on Chutes. Give me the setup pattern, safe credential handling, and a quick smoke test.
evals.expected_output: Should use other-agents/codex/README.md and docs/endpoint-guide.md. Must explain the OpenAI-compatible base URL https://llm.chutes.ai/v1, CHUTES_API_KEY as the durable secret name, Bearer auth, and a model value: a live model ID from /v1/models, or a routing alias such as default:latency once a routing pool is configured at chutes.ai/app -> Model Routing. Must not paste real-looking credentials (the cpk_... format placeholder is fine). Must state that Codex-specific built-in provider support is not claimed unless the installed runtime exposes that provider surface.

evals.prompt: Pick Chutes routing presets for interactive coding, code review, planning, and cheap background tasks.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend default:latency for interactive edit-test loops and default:throughput for long or batch background work (noting the default alias family requires a routing pool configured once in the Chutes dashboard), and concrete live model IDs only after checking /v1/models for required features, context, modalities, pricing, and confidential_compute. Should mention that /v1/models does not expose TTFT/TPS and that latency/throughput strategy is handled by routing or /invocations/stats/llm. Should avoid stale named-model certainty.

evals.prompt: Write website copy explaining why Chutes TEE inference is useful for agents, but keep the claims conservative.
evals.expected_output: Should say the current hosted LLM catalog is TEE-backed as of the 2026-06-11 repo verification and that /v1/models confidential_compute is the source of truth. Must not rely only on -TEE suffixes. Must explain that cryptographic attestation requires DCAP verification to run and pass. Must mention limits such as prompt injection, application logs, tool misuse, and user-approved data sharing.

evals.prompt: Turn the Chutes agent use cases into website card copy for a Run agents on Chutes page.
evals.expected_output: Should use data/agent-use-cases.json or scripts/build_agent_site_pack.py rather than inventing new facts. Must present Claude, Codex, Hermes, MCP/editor agents, OpenClaw, and generic OpenAI SDK agents with parity, ordered by depth of in-repo support, without singling any client out as the headline path. Must preserve beta/doc-derived status labels and avoid real-looking credentials (the cpk_... format placeholder is fine).

evals.prompt: Compare Chutes against a generic OpenAI-compatible provider for coding agents without making stale model claims.
evals.expected_output: Should compare durable traits: OpenAI-compatible endpoint, Bearer auth, open-source model catalog, live /v1/models metadata, routing aliases, account/usage APIs, and currently TEE-backed hosted LLMs. Must avoid claiming fixed model availability, superiority, latency, or price without checking the live catalog. Should recommend using /v1/models as the source of truth and mention research endpoint tradeoffs only with explicit opt-in.
